text.title: Quarterly Retainer Review Script Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agency lead cherry-picks the best chart and skips the bad one.
antipattern.root-cause: Client trust erodes when they discover the omitted metric.
antipattern.fix: Include every contractual metric; lead with bad numbers + 'what we did about it'.

antipattern.symptom: Call ends with 'we'll loop back' and no named date.
antipattern.root-cause: Renewal slips to last-minute panic or silent churn.
antipattern.fix: Force decision OR named date ≤14 days; if client refuses, treat as at-risk.

antipattern.symptom: Agency lead spends the blockers segment defending past work instead of listening.
antipattern.root-cause: Advocacy crowds out the real friction; client never feels heard.
antipattern.fix: Use Voss mirror technique; cap agency speaking time at 30% of blocker segment.

antipattern.symptom: Client opens with 'cut budget 20%' and call becomes a discount negotiation.
antipattern.root-cause: Skips data and outcomes; informed discussion never happens.
antipattern.fix: Acknowledge, defer: 'Heard — let me get through the data first; we'll come back in segment 4.'

antipattern.symptom: Client cites work done outside the retainer as 'what we're paying for'.
antipattern.root-cause: Agency lead doesn't push back live.
antipattern.fix: Live correction: 'That work was outside the retainer — billed separately. Happy to bring it inside scope going forward, that's a scale_up decision.'

antipattern.symptom: Verbal agreement; follow-up email never arrives or arrives 5 days later.
antipattern.fix: Block call-complete state until follow_up_email.sent_at is recorded ≤24h.
